nodes.name: 人类活动扰动
nodes.summary: 旅游、施工和高频活动影响繁殖或觅食。

nodes.name: 布宜諾斯艾利斯
nodes.summary: 布宜諾斯艾利斯是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括Plumbeous ibis，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

nodes.name: 栖息地破坏
nodes.summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

nodes.name: 图库曼省
nodes.summary: 图库曼省是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联22种鸟类，包括Plumbeous ibis、大巨嘴鳥、绿颊锥尾鹦鹉等22种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 科尔多瓦省 (阿根廷)
nodes.summary: 科尔多瓦省 (阿根廷)是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联11种鸟类，包括Plumbeous ibis、查科林鴞、Dinelli's doradito等11种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 农药污染
nodes.summary: 农业化学品通过水体和食物链影响鸟类生存。

nodes.name: 鹈形目
nodes.summary: 鹈形目（Pelecaniformes）是图谱中的目级分类节点，当前关联93种鸟类，如朱鹮、黑脸琵鹭、Plumbeous ibis等93种鸟类。

nodes.name: 巴西
nodes.summary: 巴西是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联854种鸟类，包括大美洲鸵、侏䳍、白腹拟䳍等854种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 帕图斯潟湖
nodes.summary: 帕图斯潟湖是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联2种鸟类，包括Plumbeous ibis、大巨嘴鳥，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 森林砍伐
nodes.summary: 原生林被砍伐导致栖息地面积和连通性下降。

nodes.name: 鹮科
nodes.summary: 鹮科（Threskiornithidae）是图谱中的科级分类节点，当前关联30种鸟类，如朱鹮、黑脸琵鹭、Plumbeous ibis等30种鸟类。

nodes.name: 安地斯山脈
nodes.summary: 安地斯山脈是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联156种鸟类，包括北山䳍、达尔文拟䳍、高山斑䳍等156种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 马托格罗索州
nodes.summary: 马托格罗索州是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联93种鸟类，包括灰穴䳍、Chaco chachalaca、蓝眼地鸠等93种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: Plumbeous ibis
nodes.summary: Plumbeous ibis（Plumbeous ibis，学名 Theristicus caerulescens），分类上属于鹈形目，并归入鹮科，在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联7个分布地点节点，常见栖息地包括湿地，主要威胁包括栖息地破坏、农药污染、森林砍伐等4项。

nodes.name: 湿地
nodes.summary: 浅水、沼泽与植被斑块组成的高生产力生态系统。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: It occurs in south-western Brazil, especially in southern Mato GrossoDubs B, 1983

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: It occurs in south-western Brazil, especially in southern Mato GrossoDubs B, 1983

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The northern Bolivian population is geographically isolated from the larger continuous population spanning the remaining part of this ibis's range. The westernmost part of its range extends into the Andean foothills in central Bolivia and Tucuman. In Argentina, it has been occasionally sighted as far south as the Cordoba and Buenos Aires provinces; but is non-native in these regions. Although this species appears to be relatively common, it is patchily distributed throughout its global range; being relatively abundant in some regions but less so in others. For example, it is endemic but actually uncommon in Rio Grande do Sul, but frequently occurs in the northern Lagoa dos Patos. It is relatively common in the Chaco of Paraguay, especially in the northern Chaco where large numbers of individuals occur throughout the expanses of freshwater, so that this species is considered emblematic of the wetland avifauna of this region.Verschuren J

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: If approached by a human, this ibis screams loudly; whereby it lowers its body, bristles its feathers and jabs its bill at the intruder. Further, juveniles have been noted to defend themselves with forward lunges of the bill. These defensive behaviours in this ibis reflect the bird's high predation risk and hence the adaptive value of such behaviours for survival. Like many other sympatric wading birds, it is likely threatened by habitat loss through burning and logging of nesting trees, construction and operation of hydroelectric dams in the Pantanal, and expansion of mining and agriculture. This bird is however not persecuted directly. The plumbeous ibis's apparent association with humans may render it particularly vulnerable to the effects of pesticides from agriculture, especially in the Taquari Basin. Additionally, pesticides together with physical disturbance to soil through agricultural machinery may reduce local availability of macroinvertebrate prey to this species (Stenert et al
